characters.physical_details: Brown-haired Hufflepuff with ink-stained fingers and a branded right palm; by the battle he has cracked nails and nosebleeds.

key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Early closest friend; friendship breaks over his refusals.
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: Organizer who bars him from resistance work.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Headmaster who recognizes traces and warns him.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Immediate bodily threat and trigger for failed interventions.

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year and secret First Art researcher; later the node's keeper.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English, school spell Latin, and basic runes; by spring can parse Meridian maintenance notation.

state_transitions.change: Gets the coffer brand and takes the Grinlow Leaves.
First amendment destabilizes his ordinary wandwork around thresholds and closures.
Great Hall contact amendment makes blows and punishment magic land off-center, leaving a ward scar and worsening wand recoil.
Learns Snape can read amendment traces and that the castle may start completing half-made conditions on its own.
Learns that a major battle at Hogwarts will whiten the breach and attract outside predatory attention.
Repairs the seam, discovers the hostile-wandwork clause, refuses it, and takes severe physical strain.
Signs himself to the node and begins first-light/last-light maintenance after the Leaves are absorbed.

characters.availability: Present all year; below the castle during the battle's decisive stretch; alive afterward.

characters.physical_details: Compact and broad-shouldered, usually in a dark plait; by late winter she has split knuckles, a limp, a swollen knee, and a strapped right hand.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Early closest friend; sees him choose deeper work over immediate need.
key_relationships.tallow: Kitchen helper who feeds and patches her.

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year, resistance courier, and crisis caregiver.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English and ordinary Hogwarts texts; no First Art literacy.

state_transitions.change: Great Hall punishment leaves visible injuries and shows her Owen can distort immediate magical violence.
Lower-west sabotage fails; Amycus makes her the corridor example.
Detention worsens her knee and hand; she sees Owen keep a prepared page for the lower door and ends the friendship.
Stays in the fight and triage network despite the limp.

characters.availability: Present through battle and aftermath; alive, but no longer Owen's friend.

characters.physical_details: Tall Ravenclaw with dark curls, wire-rimmed spectacles, and ink-black cuffs.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Bibliographic ally; witness to his refusal.
key_relationships.tallow: Tolerated scholar of books and kitchens.

characters.occupation_status: Ravenclaw sixth-year, archive-minded astronomy helper, and Owen's collaborator.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English fluently; strong on bibliography, astronomy overlays, runic annotation, and enough Meridian and founder notation to map the anchor tables.

state_transitions.change: Recognizes Owen's citation as impossible in normal printings.
Proves the citation survived in an early print lineage and becomes a full collaborator.
Maps the four anchors and realizes Owen may not choose people over repair.
Offers the 'help the living first' compromise and loses trust when he cannot promise it.
Reaches the chamber, sees the live clause, and witnesses his refusal.

characters.availability: Present through dawn; alive afterward; one of few people who knows Owen's choice below.

characters.physical_details: Solidly built, often bruised, and worn by command labor.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Dismissed student later barred from resistance work.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Headmaster he opposes as part of the regime.

characters.occupation_status: Gryffindor student and de facto in-school resistance organizer until the open battle.
characters.literacy_languages: Standard Hogwarts spell literacy; no direct First Art knowledge.

state_transitions.change: Rejects Owen's manuscript warning as unusable in wartime conditions.
Bars Owen from further resistance jobs after the corridor fiasco.
Keeps Owen off routes, meetings, and message work after the Snape summons.
Becomes open battle commander when Harry returns.

characters.availability: Present all year, central to the surface battle, alive afterward.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Student under watch because amendment traces keep scarring the school.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Nominal ally kept ignorant of old ward damage.

characters.occupation_status: Headmaster under Voldemort's regime, publicly complicit and privately running a narrower game.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in magical theory and older scholarship around amendment traditions.

state_transitions.change: Summons Owen, identifies amendment traces, and warns that the castle may start completing half-made conditions on its own.
Dead before the dawn aftermath is fully processed by students.

characters.availability: Present through the occupation; dead by dawn on 2 May 1998.

characters.physical_details: Thickset, heavy-handed, quick to flush, often smelling of sweat and drink.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Student target whose odd survivals irritate him.
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Punishment target and repeated public example.
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: Student enemy he never fully catches.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Superior he resents but nominally serves.

characters.occupation_status: Death Eater Dark Arts professor and chief student-level enforcer.
characters.literacy_languages: Ordinary magical literacy only; no understanding of the First Art.

state_transitions.change: Great Hall punishment reveals repeated contact distortions he cannot explain.
Takes Martha for exemplary punishment after the lower-west failure.
Loses power with the regime's collapse.

characters.availability: Active threat until the battle; defeated afterward.

characters.physical_details: Small, soot-darkened, singe-eared house-elf with wet working hands; deep old-work spaces hurt him.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Reluctant guide and later node-maintenance counterpart.
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Favored kitchen child who gets fed and spoken to plainly.
key_relationships.prue_hollis: Tolerated scholar who handles books and tools carefully.

characters.occupation_status: Kitchen and scullery house-elf with memory of service routes, hush boxes, and maintenance marks.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads labels, inventories, and founder-era maintenance notation better than any student.

state_transitions.change: Explains the flour-bin aperture and hush-box logic that let Owen reach C-13.
Leads Owen into the East Undercroft despite the pain it causes him.
Brings battle-night repair materials to the threshold and reports the castle's upper-story misbehavior.
Helps the node take back the Leaves and instructs Owen in continuing maintenance intervals.

characters.availability: Present in kitchens and service runs throughout; cannot comfortably enter deep live-work zones but remains involved at their threshold.

timeline.story_start: September 1997, the first Hogwarts term under Snape after the Ministry falls.
timeline.estimated_span: About eight months, from term start to dawn on 2 May 1998.

seasonal_track.season: night of 1 May 1998 through dawn of 2 May 1998

key_events.event: Owen finds the North Stacks C-13 footnote in an 1878 Binns copy.
He reaches the retired shelf-chase, opens the hush box, and takes the Grinlow Leaves.
Neville rejects Owen's warning, but Prue recognizes the citation as impossible.
The greenhouse test proves the First Art and destabilizes Owen's wandwork.
Amycus's Great Hall punishment leaves a ward scar.
The East Undercroft confirms Hogwarts as one anchor in a planetary masking system.
The lower-west sabotage fails; Martha is punished and Neville bars Owen from operations.
Owen and Prue map the four remaining anchors and Hogwarts' local branch.
Snape identifies amendment traces and warns Hogwarts may finish half-made conditions on its own.
The cracked star-lens shows a major Hogwarts battle would whiten the breach and attract predatory attention.
Harry returns; the battle begins, and Owen goes below.
Owen repairs the seam, refuses the hostile-wandwork clause, and Prue witnesses it.
key_events.timing: late night 1 May into early 2 May 1998

key_events.event: By dawn the manuscript is absorbed into the node and Owen signs for upkeep.

geography.primary_setting: Hogwarts under occupation, with ordinary school spaces above and founder-era infrastructure waking below.

key_locations.name: Hufflepuff cellar and common room
key_locations.details: Warm quarters near the kitchens; Owen's social baseline.

key_locations.name: Great Hall
key_locations.details: Occupation stage and later triage space; chapter 6 leaves a ward scar from the Hufflepuff bench toward the aisle.

key_locations.name: Library north stacks and C-13 shelf-chase
key_locations.details: Blast-damaged bay exposing obsolete shelf numerals, the retired chase, and the lead hush box.

key_locations.name: Kitchens, scullery, and service routes
key_locations.details: Main labor space and main access to old infrastructure, including the flour-bin aperture and later third-rack/third-flue descent.

key_locations.name: Greenhouse row
key_locations.details: Abandoned house for Owen's first amendment and later winter workspace for him and Prue.

key_locations.name: Lower west corridor and service press
key_locations.details: Resistance extraction route damaged by Owen's threshold work; doors, cisterns, armor, and the press answer as one passage system.

key_locations.name: East Undercroft
key_locations.details: Founder-era service chamber below the castle with the live Hogwarts anchor stone, floor rings, channels, and maintenance surfaces.

key_locations.name: Lower maintenance door and lake stair
key_locations.details: Founder-era seam below the undercroft that opens only when the site answers as transit, leading to the lakeward departure level.

key_locations.name: Departure Chamber
key_locations.details: Lakeward transit chamber with waiting benches, transport grooves, and the cracked star-lens that reads the ward skin from inside.

distances: The Hufflepuff cellar sits nearest the kitchens, letting Owen move between common room and service passages quickly.
The retired C-13 shelf-chase drops from the library north wall toward kitchen-side service space through an adult-narrow aperture.
The East Undercroft is reached from kitchen-side maintenance runs and, in battle, by the third-flue descent beyond the scullery and coal bins.
The lower maintenance door sits beneath the East Undercroft and opens into the lake stair only when the node answers as transit.
The Departure Chamber lies farther down toward the lake and opens only when the undercroft answers as transit.
The lower west corridor links the staff lavatory, classrooms, fruit portrait, and service arches near the scullery.

world_rules: 1997-1998 Hogwarts under Snape: the Carrows use class, detention, and public punishment as terror.
Movement relies on corridors, portraits, staircases, and service passages rather than Apparition.
The First Art edits categories, thresholds, and allowed relations beneath spellcasting rather than producing one obedient effect.
Live pages and tables answer only under the right conditions: coffer brand, astronomy overlays, active node, or a strong ward scar.
Amendments bind to fixed frames, thresholds, prepared surfaces, or founder stone; imprecise phrasing spreads across the chosen category.
Successful amendment scars local wards and destabilizes nearby wandwork; under enough stress the castle can complete half-made conditions on its own.
House-elves understand service marks and old routes better than students, but deep live-work zones pain them physically.
The Meridian Commonwealth evacuated Earth deliberately after building a four-anchor masking ward; Hogwarts is one anchor.
Battle-scale discharge at Hogwarts brightens the breach and attracts predatory attention toward Earth.
The siege-state Hogwarts node can suspend hostile wandwork, but it also suppresses defenders' hostile wandwork and competes with seam repair.
Once live records are worked against founder stone, a damaged node can absorb them and keep only the maintenance face.

power_structure.mechanism: Political conquest backed by murder, occupation, and the Carrows' delegated violence.

power_structure.over: Students and remaining staff at Hogwarts
power_structure.mechanism: Formal headmaster authority, surveillance, and control of access under the occupying regime.

power_structure.over: Students in class, detention, and corridor discipline
power_structure.mechanism: Direct bodily violence, humiliation, and Dark Arts instruction.
Earned trust, operational planning, and willingness to take risks first.

power_structure.over: Kitchen labor, service routes, and old maintenance knowledge near the node
power_structure.mechanism: Institutional memory, daily access, and house-elf authority inside workspaces.

power_structure.over: Movement, doors, and wand relations inside the castle under extreme stress
power_structure.mechanism: Embedded house logic that can wake, reroute, or suppress conditions when old seams are activated.

power_structure.over: The live Hogwarts node by dawn after the battle
power_structure.mechanism: Direct claim through the coffer brand, battle-night repair, and his signed maintenance obligation.

objects.name: 1878 Binns classroom copy of A History of Magic
objects.origin: Old school text preserving the removed North Stacks C-13 footnote.

objects.name: The Grinlow Leaves
objects.owner: Owen Phipps until chapter 18, then the live node itself
objects.origin: Founder-era manuscript packet hidden in the C-13 hush box.
objects.status: Recovered in chapter 2; by chapter 18 the node absorbs most of it into the seam and leaves only a maintenance face at the surface.

objects.name: Lead hush box from North Stacks C-13
objects.origin: Lead-lined coffer built to defeat cataloguing and Summoning magic.
objects.status: Forced open in chapter 2 and left damaged in the retired shelf-chase.

objects.name: Owen's ash-linen notebook
objects.origin: School notebook turned into a working copy for amendments, routes, tables, and battle notes.
objects.status: Survives, but Owen pulps the departure and shelf-route copies into the seam seal in chapter 18.

objects.name: Hogwarts anchor stone
objects.owner: No personal owner; live component of the planetary mask under Hogwarts
objects.origin: Founder-era node below the castle tied to the Meridian ward system.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 7; the western seam is banked in chapter 16 but not healed, so the node still needs maintenance.

objects.name: Departure Chamber star-lens
objects.owner: No personal owner; fixed monitoring instrument of the older system
objects.origin: Meridian transit infrastructure beneath the lake, built to read the ward skin from inside the mask.
objects.status: Activated in chapter 13; cracked but still legible enough to show breach risk.

objects.name: Founder hostile-wandwork clause
objects.owner: Embedded in Hogwarts' local node logic rather than portable
objects.origin: Dormant house-protection branch that surfaces only under siege while a claimant holds the live node.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 16; Owen leaves it unused, and it sinks again once the battle tips out of siege balance.

knowledge_state.knows: The 1878 Binns copy preserves a real North Stacks C-13 footnote.
The Grinlow Leaves are real, keyed to his branded palm, and describe First Art.
Owen's citation is impossible in normal school printings and points to removal.
Owen can distort how punishment magic and force land.
The East Undercroft holds a live anchor tied to planetary masking and departure infrastructure.
Four anchors remain active: Hogwarts, a red inland waste, a western mountain spine, and an interior-Asian salt plateau; Hogwarts also carries a local branch.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Martha, Neville, Snape, and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen is practicing amendment magic serious enough to scar wards and wake autonomous castle responses, though Snape does not know the exact manuscript or site.
Snape can recognize amendment traces, and the castle may start completing half-made conditions on its own if pushed.
The undercroft can answer as transit and open the lakeward Departure Chamber, whose star-lens reads the ward from inside.
A major Hogwarts battle will whiten the breach and attract predatory outside attention toward Earth.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone else unless Owen shares it.

knowledge_state.knows: The live node contains a founder clause that could suspend hostile wandwork across Hogwarts during the battle.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone else until Prue reaches the chamber.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen had a live way to suspend hostile wandwork while the battle was undecided and refused it.
After the battle the Leaves are part of the node; first-light and last-light checks track seep, brightness, and load, while line travel or black sweating warn of trouble.

environmental_constants: Castle cold finds wrists, ankles, and knees even with fires lit.
Occupied Hogwarts smells of soot, old paper, damp wool, and food.
The kitchens are warmer, wetter, and louder than the upper school.
The damaged north library keeps grit, dust, and splintered wood after the blast.
First Art sites feel wrong in the body: tooth-pressure, hot stone, ringing ears, nausea, thickened air, and sound through bone.
The undercroft and lakeward chambers are damp and mineral; voices reduce to thuds and pressure through stone.
During the battle, smoke and stone dust reach even the subterranean scenes.
